guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.method: For each pending trigger (a contingent enactment or a dated commencement) the layer counts the directed edges it reclassifies, split into fan-in (edges terminating in the trigger jurisdiction) and fan-out (edges originating there), derived edge-by-edge from computed_corridor_states.json. It asserts NO new facts; every count is a re-derivation of the corridor states.
data.finding: The forward map's class-level motion concentrates at the one remaining pre-regime origin (South Korea, 21 edges) and the two regime-in-transition destinations (Taiwan, 18; the United Kingdom, 8). The United States is also a class-mover (8 edges), enacted-not-commenced with a dated horizon. Two orderings must be read together: by edge count KR(21) > TW(18) > UK(8) ≈ US(8) > CN(0); by dated horizon US(≤2027-01-18, outer cap) precedes UK(2027-10-25, gazetted), while TW and KR carry no date and move no horizon. The prohibition destination (Mainland China) is insensitive to any positive trigger.
data.ranking_criteria: Ordered by breadth (edges reclassified) first; among the regime-in-transition destinations the paper's tie-breakers are timing certainty (a gazetted commencement date outranks an ungazetted one) and resolution class (a clean Category I outranks a channelled Category II). Separately, a DATED ordering ranks the two scheduled horizons (US outer cap 2027-01-18 before UK gazetted 2027-10-25). Breadth is mechanical; the tie-breakers and the dated ordering are the paper's.

ordering.note: The single highest-sensitivity jurisdiction: a both-directions trigger at the one remaining pre-regime origin. A single contingent enactment reclassifies the most edges of any pending trigger (21), lifting South Korea out of pre_regime inbound and out of Category III outbound. Under destination-first the post-trigger classes are set by each counterpart destination, not uniformly II.

ordering.timing: no gazetted date (nine subsidiary items pending)
ordering.note: Enacted at third reading 2026-06-30, not commenced. Second, not tied with the UK: its commencement moves 18 edges because two of its outbound edges (TW→CN blocked, TW→KR pre_regime) are held by their DESTINATIONS and cannot move whatever Taiwan does — a direct consequence of the destination-first correction (NF-1).

Commencement reclassifies every inbound edge from a token-holding origin from Category T to a clean Category I on a gazetted day, resolving the eight-pair caveat in full. On EDGE COUNT it ties the US (8 each); on the DATED ordering it comes SECOND, because the US horizon (outer cap 2027-01-18) falls first.

E1 CORRECTION: the United States is enacted-not-commenced, not live. GENIUS Act §20 sets the effective date at the earlier of 18 months after enactment (2027-01-18) or 120 days after final regulations; at the snapshot only proposed rules existed, so the 2027-01-18 outer cap binds. The 8 token-holder→US edges read Category T today and resolve to II at commencement. This is the FIRST of the two dated horizons — earlier than the UK's gazetted 2027-10-25.

insensitive.note: A pending enactment that hardens the Single-Currency-Stablecoin cells' binding status without moving any feasibility class — the baseline PS Act 2019 DPT gate is already in force. Accessibility (intra-regime gating), not class.

Genuinely insensitive — 0 edges. A destination prohibition dominates the origin's issuance gap, so every edge terminating in the PRC is stable across the entire pending set. §4.2's 'insensitive' claim becomes TRUE the moment NF-1 (destination-first) is fixed.

disagreement_as_finding.observation: Taiwan's inbound fan (9 edges) is mechanically one larger than the United Kingdom's (8), because Taiwan is not itself a token-holder, so all 9 token-holding origins point inbound to it, whereas the United Kingdom's own self-edge is excluded. A breadth-only ranking would place Taiwan above the United Kingdom among the regime-in-transition destinations.
disagreement_as_finding.resolution: The paper places the United Kingdom ahead on its stated tie-breakers: timing certainty (UK gazetted 2027-10-25; Taiwan ungazetted) and resolution class (UK resolves to clean Category I; Taiwan to channelled Category II). Breadth is mechanical; the tie-breakers are the paper's.
disagreement_as_finding.handling: Recorded, not hidden: the layer keeps both the mechanical fan counts and the paper's ordering, surfacing the divergence as a disagreement-as-finding, consistent with the computed-vs-authored reconciliation discipline.

provenance.note: Every count is derived edge-by-edge from computed_corridor_states.json, itself a re-derivation of the signal compose(). The layer reshapes and orders; it introduces no fact.
provenance.regenerated: v0.10.1: recomputed from the register's own frozen forward transition set. US moves from the insensitive set to an 8-edge class-mover via us-genius-act-effective (§18 gate commencement); KR fan counts and TW both-directions counts re-derived under destination-first precedence. Cross-checked two ways (scheduled==as_of_timeline; recomputed counts==ordering).

two_orderings.by_dated_horizon: US (≤ 2027-01-18, outer cap) > UK (2027-10-25, gazetted). TW and KR carry no date and move no horizon.
two_orderings.note: A forward map must report both. Edge count answers 'which pending change reclassifies the most edges'; dated horizon answers 'which dated flip arrives first'. The US horizon comes first and was omitted from the paper's §4.2 as drafted (errata E1).
